generated_oml:
// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance OccupancySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Continuous monitoring sensor for occupancy detection within building spaces, providing real-time occupancy data for operational optimization and space utilization analysis."
    DTDFVocab:producedData OccupancyDataTransmitted
]

instance TemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Environmental sensor measuring indoor temperature at critical building zones, enabling real-time climate control and energy efficiency monitoring."
    DTDFVocab:producedData TemperatureDataTransmitted
]

instance HumiditySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Environmental sensor measuring indoor humidity levels, supporting HVAC system optimization and indoor air quality management."
    DTDFVocab:producedData HumidityDataTransmitted
]

instance EnergyConsumptionSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Building energy monitoring sensor tracking real-time energy consumption across MEP systems, enabling predictive maintenance and energy cost optimization."
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnergyConsumptionDataTransmitted
]

instance EquipmentStatusSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Operational sensor monitoring HVAC, lighting, and other equipment status, providing fault detection and performance benchmarking capabilities."
    DTDFVocab:producedData EquipmentStatusDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance OccupancyDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom OccupancySensor
]

instance TemperatureDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom TemperatureSensor
]

instance HumidityDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HumiditySensor
]

instance EnergyConsumptionDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EnergyConsumptionSensor
]

instance EquipmentStatusDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EquipmentStatusSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance BimGeometricModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Foundational Building Information Model (BIM) containing geometric, spatial, and semantic building information inherited from design phases."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo BimIntegrationEnabler
]

instance BcsBehavioralModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Building Control System (BCS) model enriched with device-specific configurations including sensors, actuators, and control logic."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ModelDrivenEngineeringEnabler
]

instance ThermalPerformanceModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Physics-based thermal model derived from BIM and operational data, enabling energy simulation and optimization."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulationEngineEnabler
]

instance StructuralAnalysisModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Structural behavior model supporting dynamic analysis under operational loads, integrated with BIM for lifecycle monitoring."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulationEngineEnabler
]

instance OccupancyData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Real-time occupancy data stream from building spaces, used for space utilization analytics and predictive maintenance scheduling."
    DTDFVocab:fromData OccupancyDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataManagementEnabler
]

instance EnvironmentalData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated environmental metrics including temperature, humidity, and air quality, enabling HVAC optimization and indoor comfort analysis."
    DTDFVocab:fromData TemperatureDataTransmitted, HumidityDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataManagementEnabler
]

instance EnergyData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Historical and real-time energy consumption data across MEP systems, supporting predictive analytics and cost optimization."
    DTDFVocab:fromData EnergyConsumptionDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataManagementEnabler
]

instance EquipmentStatusData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Operational status data for building equipment, enabling fault detection, performance benchmarking, and maintenance scheduling."
    DTDFVocab:fromData EquipmentStatusDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataManagementEnabler
]

instance BimEnrichedData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Semantically enriched BIM data integrating geometric, spatial, and operational metadata for unified digital twin representation."
    DTDFVocab:fromData OccupancyData, EnvironmentalData, EnergyData, EquipmentStatusData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo BimIntegrationEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance BimIntegrationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Integration framework leveraging Industry Foundation Classes (IFC) and CityGML for semantic interoperability between BIM models and operational data."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService, VisualizationService, CollaborationService
]

instance ModelDrivenEngineeringEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Model-Driven Engineering (MDE) framework automating extraction of structured representations from BIM and generation of deployable digital twin artifacts."
    DTDFVocab:enables PredictiveAnalyticsService, OptimizationService
]

instance SimulationEngineEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Physics-based and data-driven simulation engine supporting EnergyPlus, ANSYS, and OpenSees for multi-domain performance analysis."
    DTDFVocab:enables OptimizationService, ControlAutomationService
]

instance DataManagementEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Real-time data ingestion and processing pipeline using InfluxDB, TimescaleDB, and graph databases for dynamic twin synchronization."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService, PredictiveAnalyticsService
]

instance AiMlEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Machine learning framework supporting supervised learning for failure prediction, reinforcement learning for adaptive control, and federated learning for privacy-preserving analytics."
    DTDFVocab:enables PredictiveAnalyticsService, OptimizationService
]

instance DevelopmentToolEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Digital twin development environment with Revit, NVIDIA Omniverse, and custom SDKs for model authoring and twin lifecycle management."
    DTDFVocab:enables VisualizationService, CollaborationService
]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Continuous data ingestion and validation from IoT sensors, BMS, and operational logs to create live virtual replica of physical asset."
    DTDFVocab:provides SystemStateInsight, AnomalyDetectionInsight, ComplianceTrackingInsight
]

instance PredictiveAnalyticsService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Machine learning-driven forecasting for predictive maintenance, energy demand prediction, and occupancy pattern analysis."
    DTDFVocab:provides FailureProbabilityInsight, LifecycleCostInsight, ResourceAllocationInsight
    DTDFVocab:provides PredictiveMaintenanceAction, EnergyOptimizationAction
]

instance OptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Multi-objective optimization for energy efficiency, occupant comfort, and structural integrity via simulation-based what-if analysis."
    DTDFVocab:provides DynamicReconfigurationInsight, CostBenefitAnalysisInsight
    DTDFVocab:provides SystemReconfigurationAction, RefurbishmentPlanningAction
]

instance ControlAutomationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Closed-loop control integration with physical systems to execute automated adjustments based on digital twin insights."
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeCorrectiveAction, PrescriptiveControlPolicyInsight
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Immersive 3D/4D/5D modeling with interactive dashboards and AR/VR interfaces for scenario visualization and decision support."
    DTDFVocab:provides ScenarioVisualizationInsight, CollaborativeAnnotationInsight
]

instance CollaborationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Centralized data repository with version control and domain-specific ontologies for cross-disciplinary team collaboration."
    DTDFVocab:provides ConflictResolutionInsight, KnowledgeReuseInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance SystemStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Continuous monitoring of sensor/actuator performance and system health metrics against design specifications."
]

instance AnomalyDetectionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Identification of equipment failures, energy inefficiencies, and operational deviations through real-time data analysis."
]

instance ComplianceTrackingInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Monitoring of regulatory and sustainability metrics to ensure building operations meet compliance requirements."
]

instance FailureProbabilityInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Probabilistic modeling of equipment failure risks based on historical and real-time operational data."
]

instance LifecycleCostInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Optimized lifecycle cost analysis incorporating maintenance scheduling and resource allocation strategies."
]

instance ResourceAllocationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Data-driven recommendations for energy procurement, maintenance scheduling, and operational resource distribution."
]

instance DynamicReconfigurationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Simulation-based analysis of HVAC setpoints, lighting schedules, and system configurations for optimal performance."
]

instance CostBenefitAnalysisInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Tradeoff analysis between energy efficiency, occupant comfort, and structural integrity for informed decision-making."
]

instance ScenarioVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Immersive visualization of refurbishment impacts, failure propagation scenarios, and operational optimization strategies."
]

instance CollaborativeAnnotationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Multi-disciplinary annotation capabilities for design clash resolution and knowledge sharing across AECO teams."
]

instance ConflictResolutionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Automated detection and resolution of design clashes between architectural, structural, and MEP systems."
]

instance KnowledgeReuseInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Standardized data exchange and pattern recognition across projects to facilitate best practice implementation."
]

instance PredictiveMaintenanceAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated scheduling of maintenance activities based on predicted equipment failure probabilities."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance EnergyOptimizationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Dynamic adjustment of energy consumption patterns to minimize costs while maintaining operational requirements."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance SystemReconfigurationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated reconfiguration of building systems (HVAC, lighting) based on real-time performance metrics and optimization insights."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance RefurbishmentPlanningAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Data-driven refurbishment strategy development incorporating lifecycle cost analysis and performance optimization."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance RealTimeCorrectiveAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Immediate corrective measures for fault mitigation and system stabilization based on real-time anomaly detection."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance PrescriptiveControlPolicyInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Optimized control policies for building systems derived from predictive analytics and operational feedback."
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The **virtual_to_physical_interaction** in this DT system involves **control commands** generated by the digital twin and transmitted to physical building systems for execution. These commands include **actuation signals** (e.g., HVAC adjustments, lighting control, or security system triggers) and **configuration updates** (e.g., runtime adjustments to operational parameters). The **control message formats** are standardized to ensure compatibility with integrated services, though specific formats (e.g., JSON, MQTT payloads) are not detailed. **Validation and authentication mechanisms** are implied to enforce security and prevent unauthorized modifications, aligning with the framework’s **non-functional requirements (NF_X)** for interoperability and maintainability. **Feedback systems** provide confirmation of command execution (e.g., acknowledgment signals or status updates from physical devices), while **conflict resolution** is managed through the DT Framework’s **runtime configuration management**, allowing dynamic adjustments without full redeployment. The system ensures **command execution confirmation** via bidirectional data flows, closing the loop between digital control and physical response."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The **Digital Twin constellation** in the AECO domain requires **orchestration and coordination mechanisms** to integrate **static models** (e.g., BIM, CAD) with **runtime data** (e.g., IoT sensors, operational logs) into a unified cyber-physical system. The architecture must support **component integration** across heterogeneous data sources and models, leveraging **interoperability standards** (e.g., IFC, CityGML, or domain-specific ontologies) to ensure seamless data exchange. **Resource management** is critical, particularly in large-scale deployments, where computational and storage demands must be balanced with real-time performance requirements. The system-wide coordination relies on **predictive analytics and simulation engines** to enable data-driven decision-making, though the document does not specify exact orchestration frameworks (e.g., microservices, event-driven architectures) or real-time synchronization protocols (e.g., MQTT, OPC UA)."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) lifecycle in the proposed model-driven deployment framework spans from **initial device modeling** to **runtime operation**, addressing the gap between static Building Information Modeling (BIM) representations and dynamic DT requirements. Key phases include:
  1. **Device Modeling**: Building Control Engineers define **sensors, actuators, and controllers** directly within the BIM environment, enriching static BIM models with **semantic representations** of Building Control System (BCS) components (e.g., functional requirements **F_X** in Table III). This phase establishes a **unified semantic foundation** for subsequent transformations.
  2. **Model Transformation**: Leveraging **Model-Driven Engineering (MDE) principles**, the framework extracts **structured representations** from enriched BIM models and **automatically generates deployable DT artifacts** (e.g., executable models, simulation environments) with **minimal manual intervention**.
  3. **DT Deployment**: The generated artifacts are deployed to synchronize the physical building and its digital twin, supported by **real-time data exchange capabilities** for continuous bidirectional synchronization.
  4. **Runtime Operation**: The DT operates in tandem with the physical infrastructure, enabling **interactive visualization** (immersive interfaces) and **modular service integration** (e.g., custom analytics, control logic extensions). Facility Managers interact with the twin for **monitoring and operational control**, while DT Engineers extend functionality via **custom services and plugins**.
  **Transition Management**: The framework ensures seamless transitions between phases through **standardized extension points** and **modular architecture**, allowing dynamic adaptation to evolving operational requirements. **Lifecycle governance** is implicit in the framework’s design, addressing theoretical and practical insights from research projects like **TwinLight and BOREALIS** (Section II)."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The framework addresses **model fidelity** by bridging the gap between BIM’s **static representations** and the DT’s **dynamic operational requirements**, ensuring that enriched BIM models (with device-specific configurations) are accurately transformed into deployable DT artifacts. **Validation and verification** are implicit in the **automated generation process**, where structured representations are derived from BIM with minimal manual intervention, reducing human error. **Uncertainty sources** may arise from discrepancies between BIM models and real-world BCS components, though the document does not specify quantification methods. **Error detection and correction** are supported through **real-time data exchange**, enabling continuous synchronization between physical and digital systems. **Quality assurance** is embedded in the **modular architecture**, where standardized extension points allow for integration of custom validation services or analytics to monitor DT accuracy and operational integrity."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The **dt_technical_connection** infrastructure is designed to support **heterogeneous system interoperability** and **real-time synchronization** between physical and digital components. The system employs a **modular architecture** with **standardized extension points**, enabling integration across diverse protocols (e.g., OPC UA, MQTT, or proprietary IoT standards). The **network topology** is centralized, with the DT Framework acting as a **hub-and-spoke model** to facilitate bidirectional data exchange. **Bandwidth and latency requirements** are optimized for low-overhead updates and real-time monitoring, though exact metrics (e.g., <100ms latency) are not specified. **Reliability and redundancy mechanisms** are implied to address scalability and maintainability, ensuring continuous operation even during component failures. **Security protocols** for network communication are not explicitly detailed but are inferred to align with the framework’s **non-functional requirements (NF_X)**, which emphasize accessibility and interoperability while mitigating risks in heterogeneous environments."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The **dt_hosting_deployment** infrastructure supports **multi-platform deployment models**, including **cloud, edge, and on-premise** configurations, to accommodate varying operational needs. The system’s **computational and storage requirements** are dynamically scalable, enabling adjustments from individual components to entire infrastructure systems without full redeployment. The **deployment model** leverages the DT Framework’s **runtime configuration management**, allowing for modular service integration and low-overhead updates. **Availability and reliability** are assured through the framework’s **modular architecture** and **non-functional requirements (NF_X)**, which prioritize maintainability and fault tolerance. The deployment process begins with **Building Control Engineers** using BIM tools to model devices and initiate deployment, followed by **Facility Managers** interacting with the operational DT for real-time monitoring. The system’s **scalability mechanisms** ensure seamless expansion as new services or analytics are integrated by **DT Engineers**, maintaining operational continuity throughout the building lifecycle."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system in the AECO domain must support **horizontal integration** with **external systems** through standardized **data exchange protocols** and **interoperability frameworks** to bridge fragmented models and data silos. Key dependencies include integration with **enterprise systems** (e.g., ERP, CMMS), **domain-specific tools** (e.g., BIM platforms like Revit or ArchiCAD), and **IoT/OT ecosystems** (e.g., sensor networks, PLCs). The document emphasizes the need for **cross-domain collaboration** and **real-time data synchronization**, though it does not specify exact protocols (e.g., REST APIs, GraphQL, or proprietary interfaces) or integration patterns (e.g., API gateways, middleware layers). The system’s effectiveness hinges on adhering to **industry standards** (e.g., ISO 19650, BIM Collaboration Format) to ensure compatibility with other Digital Twins or enterprise systems across the building lifecycle."
]
